title: Chorus sub-component spec
description: Machine-readable contract for a single component sub-spec (e.g. button/standard, tabs/underline). One file per sub-component. Sub-component specs live under schema/components/<family>/<sub>.spec.json and are paired with a <sub>.md authoring spec. Token values reference the three-tier system in schema/tokens/*.json — `ref.*` (raw), `sys.*` (semantic, the default tier; typography tokens like `sys.typo.label.md` resolve to a composite `{size, weight, line, tracking}` in tokens/resolved.*.json), and the reserved-but-empty `comp.*` tier. Cross-spec delegation is allowed via JSON Pointer `$ref` (e.g. `{ "$ref": "../chip/filter.spec.json#/sizing" }`) — used where one form's chrome is delegated verbatim to another's, currently button/toolbar + tabs/segmented → chip/filter. The shape here is intentionally permissive: components vary in which axes they expose (sizes vs single sizing rung, appearance vs selection, etc.), but every spec MUST identify itself (`name`, `family`, `description`, `element`) so external consumers can index it without crawling prose.

name.description: PascalCase React component name (`Button`, `Tabs`, `FormField`). Matches the export from @blind-dsai/ui.

family.description: kebab-case family slug. Matches the folder name under schema/components/ and the `family` key in schema/manifest.json.

subcomponent.description: kebab-case sub-component slug. Omit on single-spec families (badge, banner, …); required when the family folder contains more than one spec.

description.description: One-paragraph role summary — what this sub-component is for, in the same voice as the .md lead paragraph.

element.description: The DOM element the root slot renders as (`button`, `div`, `input`, `span`, …).

exportAlias.description: Optional extra PascalCase export name (or array of names) re-pointed at this spec — used to give a sub-component a shorter / more familiar runtime name (Sheet for BottomSheet, Input/SearchBar/Select for FormField sub-components) and to keep deprecated historical names alive (ChannelList for SuggestionList). The build-types generator emits each alias as `<Alias>Props = Omit<SourceProps, "variant">` + a matching component export, so consumers don't need to repeat the variant discriminator. src/index.js MUST re-export the runtime component under every alias name.

props.description: Public React props. Each entry describes one prop's type and default; the shape varies per prop kind (enum / literal / boolean / string / number / node / function).

slots.description: Named regions the component owns (label, leadingIcon, container, …). Mirrors the `## Slots` section of the .md.

intrinsic.description: When true, this slot is filled by the component itself (auto-rendered chrome: scrim, drag handle, fixed menu icon, container shell) rather than by the consumer. Validators MUST NOT flag an unprovided intrinsic slot as missing, even when `required: true`.

accepts.description: Optional whitelist of family slugs (from schema/manifest.json#/components[].family) or primitive content kinds ('text', 'icon', 'image') that may fill this slot. When present, agents composing screens MUST verify the nested component's `family` is in this list. Absence means 'any node' — primitives only, no nested family — by default.

sizes.description: Multi-rung size axis (`large` / `medium` / `small`). Each rung carries its own token bindings. Use when the component has more than one size; otherwise use `sizing` for a single rung.

sizing.description: Single fixed-size rung's token bindings (minHeight, padding, radius, labelTypo, …). Use when the component has no size axis.

appearance.description: Single-appearance binding (background, label, …). Use when the component has no appearance axis (e.g. Badge).

appearances.description: Multi-appearance axis (`primary` / `secondary` / `outlined` / `tertiary`, `default` / `error`, …). Each key carries its own container + label tokens. Mutually exclusive with `selectionStates`.

selectionStates.description: Selection axis for toggle-shaped components (`unselected` / `selected`, or filter/toggle pairs). Mutually exclusive with `appearances`.

states.description: Interaction states (`default`, `hovered`, `pressed`, `focused`, `disabled`, `active`, …). Composes with appearance via the DESIGN.md state-overlay rule. Focus ring composition lives here.

behavior.description: Observable behavior that is not a token binding — truncation rules, fullWidth semantics, adaptive width, dismissal, keyboard model.

flavors.description: Cross-cutting modifier axis that re-tones the component family (e.g. `destructive` swaps the primary palette → error palette). Mirrors the `## Flavors` / prose note in the .md. Optional.
